title: Does your State's library Act pay for libraries?
subtitle: The nineteen State Public Libraries Acts
source: Bare-act reads (rights2reads Zotero group); research/library-law ledger v0.2, cross-checked with IIHS 2018 & IFLA 2025 (RTR-WP-008). Per-capita spend: Ministry of Culture state-wise series, CAG Combined Finance & Revenue Accounts Major Head 2205 sub-head 105, 2014-15 to 2020-21 (RTR-WP-001).
caption: Nineteen States have passed a Public Libraries Act. Read from the statute text, only six fund libraries through an operative cess — Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, Kerala and Goa — and every one was enacted before 1994. Two more legislate a cess without a working rate (Haryana) or keep the machinery but rename the levy a “fee” (Uttarakhand); the remaining eleven Acts create a library system with no dedicated funding instrument at all. Each card also shows what the State actually spent per person on public libraries, 2014-15 to 2020-21 (₹/person/year, nominal), and a ranked bar places every State's average spend side by side — the cess belt near the top, the no-cess majority collapsing toward zero rupees.

config.intro: Select your State — or tap a bar below — to read what its Public Libraries Act promises, and what it actually spends per person on public libraries.
config.spendCaveat: Nominal rupees — not inflation-adjusted. In real terms the line is lower and flatter; a flat nominal line is a fall in what a reader actually gets. (The campaign's real headline, ₹4.77, is the consolidated national figure in constant 2011-12 ₹; a per-State real series is not yet published.)

data.rate: 10% surcharge on property tax (3 paise 1950 → 5 paise 1972 → 10 paise 1992). The one cess that durably raised real per-capita spend.
8% surcharge on property tax (4 paise base).
The Andhra Pradesh 1960 Act adapted; §20 library cess, same 8% regime.
6% surcharge on property tax, plus a land-revenue grant (Rules 1966).
No library cess; the Act builds a library system funded from general grants.
No cess. The Library Fund is endowments + government grants + amounts collected under rules — it lacks the cess definition every cess Act carries. (Secondary lit wrongly lists WB as a cess State.)

data.repNote: The only State Act to mandate representation for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and women on the library authorities. Written on the back of a mass library movement.

data.rate: The Act provides for a cess but sets no operative rate — “at such rate as the Government may decide.” No rate has been fixed.
A specific excise duty: ₹0.50 per proof-litre of IMFL + ₹0.50 per bulk-litre of beer, plus 1% of the education budget (not merely the “1.67%” ad-valorem gloss).
The cess machinery is kept but the levy is renamed a “fee” — the fossil clause “except the power to levy library cess” survives in the text.
No library cess (confirmed via Hindi+English re-OCR; an earlier English-only pass misread statute citations as a cess — there is no उपकर chapter).
